channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Christina Applegate talks about the joy she's found even in hard times
item.description: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Christina Applegate tells Rachel Martin about a period in her life she daydreams about.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Christina Applegate tells Rachel Martin about a period in her life she daydreams about.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Hutchison

item.title: The second death of Cesar Chavez and his legacy
item.description: Accusations of sexual abuse by the famed union leader and champion of farmworker rights Cesar Chavez broke his legacy and those who admired him.
item.content:encoded:
Accusations of sexual abuse by the famed union leader and champion of farmworker rights Cesar Chavez broke his legacy and those who admired him.

(Image credit: Les Lee/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gallardo

item.title: Why is the 'Bachelorette' canceled? A guide to the Taylor Frankie Paul controversy
item.description: Taylor Frankie Paul rose to fame on The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives, then filmed a season of The Bachelorette. But it won't air as planned because of resurfaced domestic violence allegations.
item.content:encoded:
Taylor Frankie Paul rose to fame on The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives, then filmed a season of The Bachelorette. But it won't air as planned because of resurfaced domestic violence allegations.

(Image credit: Mike Coppola)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Martial arts star Chuck Norris dies at 86
item.description: Norris karate chopped and kickboxed his way through more than a dozen action films, before leaping to TV in Walker, Texas Ranger.
item.content:encoded:
Norris karate chopped and kickboxed his way through more than a dozen action films, before leaping to TV in Walker, Texas Ranger.

(Image credit: Jeff Golden)
item.dc:creator: Bob Mondello

item.title: Ryan Gosling and a cute alien team up to save humanity in 'Project Hail Mary'
item.description: The story is fundamentally hopeful, just like Andy Weir's The Martian.
item.content:encoded:
The story is fundamentally hopeful, just like Andy Weir's The Martian.

(Image credit: Jonathan Olley)
item.dc:creator: Linda Holmes

item.title: 10 tried-and-true methods to stay off your phone, according to our readers
item.description: We asked our audience to share the creative ways they limit their own phone use. They range from the practical (keep your phone in another room) to the creative (pair your phone with a fun paperback).
item.content:encoded: We asked our audience to share the creative ways they limit their own phone use. They range from the practical (keep your phone in another room) to the creative (pair your phone with a fun paperback).
item.dc:creator: Mika Ellison

item.title: How Season 1 of 'Jury Duty' put James Marsden's improv chops on trial
item.description: Mardsen starred in the original Jury Duty, an experimental show about a man who becomes part of a staged fake jury. Now the series is back in a new setting. Originally broadcast May 2, 2023.
item.content:encoded: Mardsen starred in the original Jury Duty, an experimental show about a man who becomes part of a staged fake jury. Now the series is back in a new setting. Originally broadcast May 2, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Sam Briger

item.title: 'Project Hail Mary' is a space comedy that comes off as glib and earthbound
item.description: Ryan Gosling plays an astronaut in this derivative, carefully manufactured crowd-pleaser; Project Hail Mary doesn't feel like storytelling so much as mechanical engineering.
item.content:encoded: Ryan Gosling plays an astronaut in this derivative, carefully manufactured crowd-pleaser; Project Hail Mary doesn't feel like storytelling so much as mechanical engineering.
item.dc:creator: Justin Chang

item.title: Afroman prevails in cops' music video defamation suit after a brief but viral trial
item.description: The "Because I Got High" rapper made waves in 2023 with the album and song "Lemon Pound Cake," using home video to mock a police raid on his Ohio home. The deputies lost their civil suit against him.
item.content:encoded: The "Because I Got High" rapper made waves in 2023 with the album and song "Lemon Pound Cake," using home video to mock a police raid on his Ohio home. The deputies lost their civil suit against him.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Singer Jill Scott is doing what she wants: 'Everything has led me to this place'
item.description: The Grammy-winning singer describes herself as a "53-year-old woman who is maneuvering her career the way she wants to, how she wants to." Scott's new album is To Whom This May Concern.
item.content:encoded:
The Grammy-winning singer describes herself as a "53-year-old woman who is maneuvering her career the way she wants to, how she wants to." Scott's new album is To Whom This May Concern.

(Image credit: Kennedi Carter)
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley
